mission: round 3 pass 1/6 — SCALE PROFILE (measurement-only); engine only ever profiled on 11-33s clips, real use case is 2h call recordings

scenarios.tiny_7200_seq: tiny.en, full 2h gerard call (7267s), whisper-cpp-native sequential

spans: mel encoder_window cross_kv decoder_prefill decode_loop model_parse model_weights version_tag backend_run; cumulative_ms field = per-CALL elapsed (mis-named), at_ms = timeline offset at span end. IF backend emits NO backend_run span.
metric: single-pass /usr/bin/time -l wall+user+sys+peak RSS; per-window span distributions; span ratios (load-robust)
cpu: Apple M4 Pro, 14 cores (10P+4E), 64 GB, macOS 26.2
discipline: HEAVY ambient host load throughout (load avg 12->100+ from concurrent agent swarm + the long perf runs sharing cores). The 2h tiny run + the first large/IF batch were run CONCURRENTLY (load 50-100) so their ABSOLUTE wall/ms-per-tok are inflated; within-run span RATIOS and drift ratios are load-robust and are what conclusions rest on. The clean_tiny600_* and clean_large600_* pairs were run one-at-a-time (load ~16-30) and are the trustworthy seq-vs-IF numbers.
code_change: NONE. Existing perf_span instrumentation was sufficient; no measurement additions needed.
